title: Compose Specification, Services, Projects, Dependencies, and Lifecycle · Container Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective validated multi-service Compose application model; isolate the first causal fault in compose specification, services, projects, dependencies, and lifecycle and preserve a regression check before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in validated multi-service Compose application model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Explain the current Compose Specification and service, network, volume, config, secret, project, and optional implementation boundaries.
Resolve project name, working directory, files, includes, merges, interpolation, environment sources, and canonical config output.
Define image or build source, command, entrypoint, environment, user, working directory, health, resources, and metadata per service.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Compose Spec Model
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug three-service project has hidden interpolation, name collisions, start-order assumptions, a mutable image, and teardown that may delete intake data. Render the model, dry-run lifecycle, and prove preservation. Competency probe: Explain the current Compose Specification and service, network, volume, config, secret, project, and optional implementation boundaries.

Inspect the Docker Compose Spec Model trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Compose Spec Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain the current compose specification and service, network, volume, config, secret, project, and optional implementation boundaries. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Compose Spec Model, keep Docker Compose Spec Model connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Compose Up Down State

Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-compose-up-down-state-1r8m1m". Tailor its compose.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-compose-up-down-state-1r8m1m
  - competency: docker-compose-up-down-state
    artifact: compose.yaml
    decision: compose up down state
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: multi-tenant build farm using an isolated BuildKit driver
    fault-injection: deny outbound DNS after initial service discovery
    retained-proof: redacted configuration render and secret-rotation confirmation
    case-key: 1r8m1m
    change: |
      services:
      api:
      image: registry.example.test/intake@sha256:verified
      depends_on:
      db:
      condition: service_healthy
      volumes:
      intake-data:
    verify: docker compose config --quiet && docker compose --dry-run up --build
    failure: Reject start order as readiness proof, implicit project identity, floating images, or teardown that silently deletes required volumes.
    repair: Render the merged model, pin identities, encode health dependencies, inspect the dry run, and document down versus down --volumes.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
